name: Gulf Breeze
flavorHeadline: Low chipotle smoke rolling over roasted tomato, sharp serrano sting, brightened by apple cider vinegar
heatDescriptor: The storm builds — smoke, depth, and your first real tingle
jarImageAlt: Jar of Tomato Warning Gulf Breeze EF-2 salsa with a burnt orange label
productStory: The barometer drops. The sky turns that particular shade of gray-green, and the air goes heavy with intention. Gulf Breeze is the first signal that the atmosphere is organizing into something purposeful. Roasted tomatoes carry low, rolling chipotle smoke — the kind that hits the back of your throat before your tongue catches up. Sharp serrano peppers deliver the first real strike of heat, a clean sting that wakes the whole palate. Then apple cider vinegar cuts through with a brightness that keeps you reaching back for more. 1,500 to 5,000 SHU. You will notice this. You will want to.
pairings: Slow-pulled pork on a brioche bun
Smoked brisket off the cutting board
A cold lager — the colder the better
